title: [perf] Packed/two-stage communication-avoiding bidiag after fused-step keep
description: Follow-up from frankenscipy-8l8r1.57. The fused Golub-Kahan step was kept with same-process RCH A/B on vmi1227854: workspace_reference_ms=297.211246, fused_step_ms=219.828076, speedup=1.352017x, digest 0x90cdd3f8f71ed2c1 unchanged, public golden SHA 1cdd3658c6caef8dec9fc58fa7e12b8d5c90151e2f93df91ffe2fcf862c16225 unchanged. Reprofile still identifies 1024x512 bidiagonal reduction as the residual core. Next primitive must be fundamentally different from TSQR, QR-first, compact-WY replay, thread fanout, scalar DLABRD recurrence, and the fused-step micropanel. Attack a packed/two-stage communication-avoiding bidiagonalization route with reusable panel buffers and GEMM-shaped far updates in pure safe Rust. Required pass 1: fresh RCH baseline/reprofile first. Required proof: reconstruction, rank/order/tie/sign policy, public golden SHA, and same-worker benchmark. Keep only Score >= 2.0; target ratio >=2.0x over current fused-step reducer.

dependencies.title: [perf][no-gaps] Close ALL vs-upstream perf gaps in pure safe Rust — no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA linkage
